title: Per-Mode Leaderboards with Stats
content:
## Overview

The game supports persistent leaderboards with separate rankings for single-player, two-player, bot-versus-bot, and a hidden rainbow mode. Scores are stored in browser local storage across sessions. The game-over screen shows a stat summary and conditionally prompts for a name; a dedicated leaderboard screen lets players browse and clear results by mode.

## Data Layer (`src/leaderboard.ts`)

- Stores up to **10 entries per mode** in browser local storage.
- Sort order: score descending → longest snake descending → survival time descending.
- Supported modes: `'single'`, `'pvp'`, `'bvb'`, `'rainbow'`. The `'rainbow'` mode carries the display label `🌈 Rainbow Snake` and keys off `snake-leaderboard-v1:rainbow` automatically, reusing all existing storage and ranking machinery.
- Exposes functions to read, add, and clear entries.
- Exposes a **qualification check** that determines whether a new score would crack the top ten before any prompt is shown to the player.

## Score Routing (`src/main.ts`)

A `leaderboardTargetMode()` helper determines which board receives a given run's score. If the run is flagged as a rainbow game, it redirects to `'rainbow'` regardless of the underlying game mode. All three scoring paths — single-player prompt, PvP prompt, and both auto-recorded Bot vs Bot entries — go through this helper, so rainbow scores **never appear on the standard boards**.

## Game-Over Screen (`src/main.ts`, `index.html`)

### Stat Summary
At the end of every run, the game-over screen displays:
- **Longest snake length** reached during the run.
- **Survival time**, formatted as a human-readable string (e.g. `"2m 05s"`) via a `formatDuration` helper.
- Two-player games show stats for both players.

Game start time and per-player maximum snake length are tracked in `src/main.ts`, reset at the start of every new game, and updated on each move. Stats are surfaced only at game-over (not mid-game) to keep the in-game UI clean.

### Name-Entry Prompt
- **Single-player and two-player modes**: the inline name-entry form appears only when the score qualifies for the top 10. Prompting unconditionally would feel hollow for low scores and devalue the leaderboard.
- **Bot-versus-bot mode**: both bots' results are recorded automatically using their display names, with no prompt. Bot runs are deterministic and unattended; there is no human present to type a name.
- **Rainbow mode**: follows the same prompt logic as the underlying game mode (human-played runs prompt if qualifying; bot-vs-bot runs auto-record), but scores route to the rainbow board via `leaderboardTargetMode()`.
- The player's last-used name is pre-filled in the input on subsequent prompts, reducing friction for returning players.
- After saving, a confirmation line shows the achieved rank.

### Bot-Assisted Run Exclusion
A `botEverActive` boolean flag in `src/main.ts` tracks whether the bot was active **at any point** during a run:
- Initialized at run start to match whether the bot is already enabled (so a run begun with the bot on is immediately disqualified).
- Set to `true` the moment the bot is switched on during a run; **never reset to `false` mid-run**.
- Reset only when a new run begins.
- The end-of-game leaderboard check gates the name prompt on `botEverActive` rather than the instantaneous `botEnabled` state, so toggling the bot off before dying does not restore leaderboard eligibility.

This replaces an earlier point-in-time check that allowed players to use the bot mid-run and disable it before death to appear on the human leaderboard.

## Leaderboard Screen (`index.html`, `src/main.ts`)

- A dedicated screen with **three always-visible tabs**: Single Player, Two Player, Bot vs Bot.
- A **fourth tab, `🌈 Rainbow Snake`**, is hidden by default and revealed only once the rainbow board contains at least one entry. Visibility is re-evaluated every time the leaderboard screen opens via `updateRainbowTabVisibility()` in `src/main.ts`. The tab starts `hidden` in `index.html`.
- After a rainbow run, the "View Leaderboard" button opens directly to the newly revealed rainbow tab.
- Clearing the rainbow board re-hides the tab and falls back to the Single Player tab. There is no separate unlock flag; visibility is derived purely from whether the board has entries, keeping the reveal stateless and self-healing.
- Each tab renders a table with columns: rank, name, score, longest snake, survival time, and — for multiplayer modes — result and opponent. The rainbow board collects runs from all game modes, so its Result and Opponent columns are shown based on what entries actually carry rather than a fixed mode assumption, avoiding empty columns for single-player entries or hidden result columns for PvP entries on the same board.
- A **"Clear This Board"** button resets entries for the selected mode independently.
- A **"View Leaderboard"** shortcut is available on the game-over screen.

## Keyboard Input Guard (`src/main.ts`)

The keyboard handler checks whether the focused element is a text input, textarea, or select before processing any game key bindings. If the user is typing in a form field, all game shortcuts (including Enter to restart and directional keys) are suppressed for that keypress. The check is at the top of the handler so it covers all current and future shortcuts uniformly.

## Files

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `src/leaderboard.ts` | Data layer: storage, qualification check, read/add/clear; defines `'rainbow'` mode |
| `src/main.ts` | Game-over logic, stat tracking, `botEverActive` flag, `leaderboardTargetMode()` helper, `updateRainbowTabVisibility()`, keyboard guard, leaderboard screen wiring |
| `index.html` | Game-over stat/prompt markup, leaderboard screen and tab structure (rainbow tab starts hidden) |
| `src/style.css` | Leaderboard and prompt styles, including `.leaderboard-prompt.hidden` for class-toggled visibility |
